hero.role: Senior fullstack engineer · 5+ years · Remote from Brazil
hero.sub: I help companies turn ideas into clean, fast and reliable products — from the first sketch to the version that ships and grows. Equally comfortable shaping the architecture, writing the code, and talking to the people who use it.

bigNumbers.title: Six years of building things that don't break.

stacks.label: Languages and frameworks in daily use

countries.label: Countries with clients I've worked with

about.title: A builder who sweats the details and ships the work.
bio: I'm Marco — most people online know me as maarkn. I'm a senior fullstack engineer based in Goiânia, Brazil, working with clients and teams around the world.

I started writing software professionally in 2019 and never really stopped. Over the years I moved from frontend trainee to leading the architecture of products used by thousands — VoIP platforms, fintech apps, real-estate assistants, museum sites, agronomy chatbots, and a few things I can't talk about yet.

What I really enjoy is being involved end-to-end: understanding what people actually need, designing how the system should behave, writing the code that makes it real, and staying close while it goes live and grows. I care about clean architecture, but I care more about software that works, doesn't surprise the team that maintains it, and gives the user a smooth ride.
When I'm not coding, I'm usually studying — currently improving my English to relocate abroad, and exploring how AI agents can be useful day-to-day instead of just impressive in demos.

values.title: End-to-end ownership
values.body: From the first conversation to the version running in production. I don't hand things off and disappear.

values.title: Clear, plain communication
values.body: I translate between business and code. Stakeholders get straight answers, not jargon.

values.title: Pragmatic over perfect
values.body: I ship the simplest thing that solves the problem, then iterate when reality pushes back.

values.title: Respect for the next person
values.body: Code is read more than it's written. I leave the codebase a little better than I found it.

freelance.summary: Building end-to-end products as a freelancer: a refactored Flutter app with in-app purchases, several WhatsApp assistants powered by LLMs, a carbon-credit reporting platform, a real-estate chatbot for Miami, and a few security and performance audits.

nectar.summary: Led the architecture of a VoIP product integrated with the company CRM. Designed billing rules, real-time provisioning, call recording and transcription, multitenancy and team management.

sevencred.summary: Built a fintech API for FCO loans with a microservices mindset, a Flutter app for applications, a Ghost-based portal, and crawlers ingesting data from public Brazilian gov APIs.

ecto.summary: Migrated Google reviews ingestion from batch to real-time events. Led two junior developers, set the code-review process, and shipped a subscription system to onboard small clients.

vendorhub.summary: Drove UI standardization, refactored the core product registration flow, and built a Chrome extension to analyze Mercado Livre ad performance directly inside the marketplace.

ecto1.summary: Shipped features on a Google My Business listings platform, managed deployments, and improved a few Gatsby blogs along the way.

collectgram1.summary: Built referral and subscription flows, fixed bugs and grew the admin dashboard of a collection-management product.

collectgram0.summary: Where it all started: a Vue.js marketplace dashboard and email marketing templates with Pug and Sass.
